subject: Gaming
title: Emulation: Legal Gray Zones
description: A quick, practical guide to Emulation: Legal Gray Zones.

hook.text: Emulators are legal. ROMs of games you don't own aren't. The gray zone between them confuses everyone.

buildup.text: Courts ruled in 1999 that emulators themselves are legal. The Sony v. Connectix case established this.

discovery.text: Downloading ROMs of copyrighted games is illegal. But making your own backup copy exists in a legal gray area.

twist.text: Nintendo shuts down ROM sites while selling the same old games at high prices. Fans call it greed, not protection.

climax.text: Emulation keeps gaming history alive while companies fail to. The legal and ethical lines rarely align.

punchline.text: Emulators are legal. ROMs aren't. History lives in the gray zone.

quiz.question: What did the Sony v. Connectix case establish about emulators?
